title: Authorize Stage A manual creation of example agent core files
description: The Clothing Advice Agent and Daily Planning Agent core files (planner.py, runtime.py, __init__.py) were created manually as Stage A infrastructure work before the governed pipeline was operational. This proposal authorizes that deviation retroactively and documents that only helpers.py and labels.py are claimed as pipeline-generated. The core agent files are classified as Stage A infrastructure — they provide the agent runtime skeleton that the governed pipeline later evolves.
rule_reference: Rule 2 — Stage A Infrastructure vs Stage B Governed Generation
justification: The governed self-evolution pipeline was not operational at the time of initial agent creation. The pipeline was built iteratively using these agents as test targets. Core agent files (planner.py, runtime.py, __init__.py) provide deterministic fixture-based runtime shells — they contain no business logic beyond parsing fixture input and returning structured output. All subsequent evolution (helpers.py, labels.py, get_recommendation_label, validate_task_priority) was done through the governed pipeline. The provenance_record.json for each agent clearly separates Stage A from Stage B files.

changed_files.change: Package init — Stage A manual creation
Agent runtime shell — Stage A manual creation
Agent planner with recommendation logic — Stage A manual creation (later evolved by pipeline)
Package init — Stage A manual creation
Agent runtime shell — Stage A manual creation
Agent planner with planning logic — Stage A manual creation (later evolved by pipeline)

approval_evidence: All 6 files are deterministic fixture-based runtime shells with no external dependencies
No governance bypass, policy bypass, or sandbox bypass logic
All subsequent agent evolution was performed through the governed pipeline
Provenance records clearly separate Stage A from Stage B files
Files are in examples/ directory (non-L0, non-protected path)
note: Retroactive authorization. The governed pipeline did not exist at the time of initial creation. Pipeline was built iteratively using these agents as targets.
